engineGuide.h2: Ford Tourneo Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Ford Tourneo across all generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 0L Diesel
entries.history: PSA DW10 engine co-developed with Ford, used in early Tourneo Custom models. Known for robust bottom end but problematic emissions components.
compatibleVariants: Tourneo Custom, Tourneo Custom Zetec, Tourneo Custom Titanium, Tourneo Custom 9-Seat, Tourneo Custom Wheelchair Accessible
commonFailures: EGR valve and cooler clogging (typically 60,000-90,000 miles)
DPF regeneration failure leading to limp mode
entries.cta: Get quotes for DW10 Ford Tourneo engine replacement

entries.title: 2L Diesel
entries.history: Larger displacement PSA-derived unit offering higher torque output. Discontinued after 2016 as Ford shifted to in-house EcoBlue range.
compatibleVariants: Tourneo Custom, Tourneo Custom Zetec, Tourneo Custom Titanium, Tourneo Custom Wheelchair Accessible
entries.cta: Get quotes for DW12 Ford Tourneo engine replacement

entries.title: 0L Diesel
entries.history: Ford’s in-house EcoBlue family introduced to meet Euro 6 emissions standards. Replaced PSA-derived units with cleaner, more efficient design.
compatibleVariants: Tourneo Custom, Tourneo Custom EcoBlue, Tourneo Custom AWD, Tourneo Custom 9-Seat, Tourneo Custom Wheelchair Accessible
entries.cta: Get quotes for XXFA Ford Tourneo engine replacement

entries.title: 0L Diesel
entries.history: Updated Euro 6d-compliant version of the EcoBlue. Features improved AdBlue injection and revised calibration for lower emissions.
compatibleVariants: Tourneo Custom EcoBlue, Tourneo Custom AWD, Tourneo Custom 9-Seat
entries.cta: Get quotes for TXWA Ford Tourneo engine replacement

entries.title: 0L Diesel (AWD)
entries.history: High-output EcoBlue variant paired with intelligent all-wheel-drive system. Tuned for passenger-carrying duties requiring extra traction.
compatibleVariants: Tourneo Custom EcoBlue AWD, Tourneo Custom 9-Seat AWD
entries.cta: Get quotes for BCFA Ford Tourneo engine replacement

entries.title: 5L Diesel
entries.history: Entry-level diesel for Tourneo Connect. PSA DV5 engine shared with Citroën and Peugeot partners. Balanced economy and adequate performance.
compatibleVariants: Tourneo Connect, Tourneo Connect Zetec, Tourneo Connect Titanium, Tourneo Active
commonFailures: Timing chain stretch (noisy on cold start)
entries.cta: Get quotes for DV5C Ford Tourneo engine replacement

entries.title: 5L Diesel
entries.history: Reliability-updated version of the DV5C with revised timing components. Produced for later Tourneo Connect models.
compatibleVariants: Tourneo Connect, Tourneo Connect Titanium, Tourneo Connect Sport
entries.cta: Get quotes for BHDB Ford Tourneo engine replacement

entries.title: 6L Diesel
entries.history: Early generation small diesel fitted to first Tourneo Connect models. Simpler design than later common-rail units but prone to specific age-related issues.
commonFailures: Oil sludge buildup from long service intervals
entries.cta: Get quotes for HHDA Ford Tourneo engine replacement

entries.title: 0L Petrol
entries.history: Award-winning three-cylinder turbocharged engine. Wet belt timing system runs inside the engine oil - pioneering design that later became a known failure point.
compatibleVariants: Tourneo Connect, Tourneo Connect Zetec, Tourneo Connect Titanium, Tourneo Connect Sport
commonFailures: Wet belt degradation blocking oil pickup (typically 60,000-80,000 miles)
Catastrophic engine seizure if belt fails
entries.cta: Get quotes for M1JA Ford Tourneo engine replacement

entries.title: 0L Petrol (Start-Stop)
entries.history: Larger displacement EcoBoost for facelifted Tourneo Connect. Delivers stronger performance while retaining wet belt timing system.
compatibleVariants: Tourneo Connect, Tourneo Connect Titanium, Tourneo Connect Sport
commonFailures: Wet belt degradation (same design as 1.0L variant)
entries.cta: Get quotes for M1JA 1.5L Ford Tourneo engine replacement

entries.title: 5L Petrol Hybrid
entries.history: Naturally aspirated Atkinson-cycle petrol engine paired with electric motor. Plug-in hybrid system offers up to 35 miles electric range for zero-emission passenger duty.
compatibleVariants: Tourneo Custom PHEV, Tourneo Custom Plug-In Hybrid
entries.cta: Get quotes for Duratec 2.5L Ford Tourneo PHEV engine replacement

commonProblems.h2: Ford Tourneo Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Ford Tourneo range, particularly the 1.0 EcoBoost petrol and 2.0 TDCi diesel units, generates significant replacement demand in the UK due to well-documented timing belt and emissions system failures. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EnginesMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Below, we break down the most common Tourneo engine failures, what repair actually costs at a dealer versus a specialist, and when replacement is the only sensible option.

problems.h4: Ford 1.0 EcoBoost Wet Belt Failure - The Single Biggest Tourneo Petrol Engine Killer
problems.affectedModels: Ford Tourneo Connect 1.0 EcoBoost (2014-2022, M1JA, M1JJ)
problems.rootCause: The wet timing belt runs inside the engine oil. Over time, the belt degrades, shedding rubber particles that block the oil pickup strainer. Oil starvation then destroys the engine - often without prior warning.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Full timing belt kit, water pump, oil pump belt, fresh oil and filter
repairOptions.longevity: 60,000-80,000 miles - only viable BEFORE belt fails

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply and fit of rebuilt EcoBoost engine with upgraded belt components
repairOptions.longevity: 12-month unlimited mileage warranty - full peace of mind

problems.recommendation: If your Tourneo Connect EcoBoost is running normally and has no oil pressure warnings, get the wet belt replaced preventatively at 60,000 miles or 6 years - specialist pricing makes this far more economical than dealer rates. If you already have low oil pressure, sludge in the sump, or a seized engine, replacement is the only realistic option. A rebuilt unit with upgraded components costs less than a dealer's catastrophic failure repair.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt 1.0 EcoBoost engine prices for your Ford Tourneo Connect

problems.h4: Ford 2.0 TDCi EGR Cooler Failure - The Diesel Tourneo Custom's Silent Killer
problems.affectedModels: Ford Tourneo Custom 2.0 TDCi (2012-2019, DW10, BNBA)
problems.rootCause: The EGR cooler becomes clogged with carbon deposits, restricting exhaust gas recirculation. Coolant then fails to flow correctly, leading to overheating and potential cooler rupture - which can hydrolock the engine.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Chemical cleaning of EGR valve only
repairOptions.longevity: Short-term fix - may return within 10,000 miles

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replacement EGR cooler, gaskets, coolant flush
Supply and fit of rebuilt DW10/BNBA engine with EGR system addressed

problems.recommendation: If you catch EGR issues early with just the valve clogging, a specialist clean buys time. Once the cooler itself fails or coolant loss is detected, replace the cooler immediately - ignoring it risks engine seizure. On a Tourneo Custom worth £4,000-£7,000, a full engine replacement only makes sense if the vehicle is otherwise immaculate or a wheelchair conversion.
problems.cta: Get quotes for DW10 EGR cooler replacement and rebuilt engine options

problems.h4: Ford 2.0 TDCi DPF Regeneration Failure - Limp Mode Nightmare for School Run Operators
problems.affectedModels: Ford Tourneo Custom 2.0 TDCi, 2.2 TDCi (2012-2019, DW10, DW12)
problems.rootCause: Short journeys and stop-start passenger transport prevent the DPF from completing active regenerations. Soot builds up, backpressure increases, and the ECU forces limp mode. Forced regenerations eventually fail, and the DPF becomes irreversibly blocked.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Diagnostic tool forced regen while driving
repairOptions.longevity: Only works if DPF not severely blocked

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply and fit of rebuilt engine with new/cleaned DPF
repairOptions.longevity: Full emissions compliance for ULEZ areas

problems.recommendation: If your Tourneo Custom is in limp mode with a DPF warning, start with a specialist forced regen - it's cheap and often works. If that fails, off-car cleaning is the next step. Only consider a full engine replacement if the DPF blockage has caused turbo or injector damage, which is common on high-mileage passenger fleet vehicles. For school run and shuttle operators, fitting a rebuilt engine with emissions system addressed is often more cost-effective than constant DPF repairs.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt DW10 engine prices with DPF solutions for your Tourneo Custom

problems.h4: Ford 1.5 TDCi DV5C Injector Failure - Rough Idle and Cold Start Misery
problems.affectedModels: Ford Tourneo Connect 1.5 TDCi (2014-2022, DV5C, BHDB)
problems.rootCause: Injector nozzles become clogged with carbon or suffer internal solenoid wear. Symptoms include rough idling, poor cold starting, increased fuel consumption, and occasional misfiring - often mistaken for diesel contamination .

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Ultrasonic cleaning and flow testing of all injectors
Replace faulty injector, recalibrate via diagnostics
Supply and fit of rebuilt DV5C/BHDB engine
repairOptions.longevity: 12-month warranty - includes all four injectors calibrated

problems.recommendation: Start with injector cleaning and testing at a diesel specialist - many owners report this resolves rough running without replacement . If one injector is confirmed faulty, replace just that unit. Only consider a full engine replacement if multiple injectors have failed or the engine has suffered consequential damage (e.g., cylinder wash, bore scoring). The 1.5 TDCi is otherwise robust, so preserve the original engine where possible.
problems.cta: Get quotes for DV5C injector repair and rebuilt engine options

problems.h4: Ford Tourneo Turbocharger Failure - Power Loss and Blue Smoke from Exhaust
problems.affectedModels: Ford Tourneo Custom 2.0 TDCi, 2.2 TDCi (2012-2019, DW10, DW12); Tourneo Connect 1.6 TDCi (HHDA)
problems.rootCause: Variable geometry turbo vanes stick due to carbon buildup from short journeys. Oil seals fail, allowing engine oil into the intake and exhaust - producing blue smoke. In severe cases, the turbo disintegrates and sends debris through the engine.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Exchange turbo unit, oil feed pipes, gaskets
Supply and fit of rebuilt engine with new turbo

problems.recommendation: If you catch turbo failure early (just whining noise or occasional blue smoke), a specialist turbo replacement is cost-effective. However, if the turbo has disintegrated, metal debris will have circulated through the engine - replacement is the only safe option. On high-mileage passenger fleet vehicles, we recommend a rebuilt engine with a new turbo for complete peace of mind.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt DW10/DW12 engine prices with new turbo included

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Ford Tourneo
finalCta.paragraph: A failed engine doesn't have to mean the end of your Tourneo. Whether you're dealing with EcoBoost wet belt failure, TDCi EGR/DPF issues, or turbo failure, EnginesMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK Ford Tourneo specialists. Every rebuilt engine supplied through our network includes upgraded internal components addressing the original failure point, a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty, and nationwide delivery with supply & fit options available.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Ford Tourneo Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EnginesMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Ford Tourneo and engine code. Every engine supplied through EnginesMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineSizes.h2: Ford Tourneo Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant
engineSizes.intro: Every displacement fitted to the Ford Tourneo across all UK generations is listed below, with compatible engine codes, years, power outputs and common failure notes.

items.title: Ford Tourneo 1.5L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 1.5L TDCi unit, derived from the PSA DV5 family, became the entry-level diesel for the Tourneo Connect from 2014. It balanced reasonable economy with adequate torque for passenger duties, replacing the older 1.6L unit in the range. Later BHDB variants received reliability updates to address early timing chain concerns.
items.cta: Get quotes for Ford Tourneo 1.5L diesel engines

items.title: Ford Tourneo 1.6L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 1.6L TDCi was the original small diesel fitted to the first-generation Tourneo Connect from 2002. Based on the PSA DV6 platform, this simpler common-rail unit lacks the AdBlue complexity of later engines but suffers from age-related injector and turbo issues on higher-mileage passenger fleet vehicles.
commonFailurePoints: oil sludge from extended service intervals
items.cta: Get quotes for Ford Tourneo 1.6L diesel engines

items.title: Ford Tourneo 2.0L Diesel Engines
items.description: The backbone of the Tourneo Custom diesel range across two generations. Early DW10/BNBA Duratorq units (2012-2019) are PSA-derived and ubiquitous across the van-derived MPV sector. Later XXFA/TXWA EcoBlue units (2019 onwards) are Ford’s in-house Euro 6 design, offering more power but adding AdBlue complexity. Both displacements share the same 2.0L capacity but represent completely different engine families with distinct failure profiles.
items.cta: Get quotes for Ford Tourneo 2.0L diesel engines

items.title: Ford Tourneo 2.2L Diesel Engines
items.description: The high-torque option for early Tourneo Custom models, the 2.2L DW12 unit shares its PSA heritage with the 2.0L DW10 but offers stronger low-end pull for heavier passenger loads. Discontinued after 2016 when Ford transitioned to the in-house EcoBlue range, it remains sought-after for its simpler emissions architecture compared to later AdBlue-equipped units.
items.cta: Get quotes for Ford Tourneo 2.2L diesel engines

items.title: Ford Tourneo 1.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: Ford’s award-winning three-cylinder EcoBoost turbo engine became the petrol option for the Tourneo Connect from 2014. Compact and surprisingly punchy, it delivers petrol refinement with diesel-like torque. However, the wet belt timing system running inside the engine oil has proven to be a high-mileage failure point, with belt degradation blocking oil pickups and causing catastrophic seizure on many passenger vehicles.
commonFailurePoints: Wet belt degradation blocking oil pickup
items.cta: Compare Ford Tourneo 1.0L petrol engine prices

items.title: Ford Tourneo 1.5L Petrol Engines
items.description: The larger-displacement EcoBoost fitted to facelifted Tourneo Connect models from 2022. Sharing the same wet belt architecture as the 1.0L unit but with an extra cylinder, it delivers stronger performance for fully loaded passenger duties. The wet belt remains the primary long-term durability concern on these later petrol units.
items.cta: Compare Ford Tourneo 1.5L petrol engine prices

groups.title: Ford Tourneo Hybrid / Electrified Engine Sizes

items.title: Ford Tourneo 2.5L PHEV Engines
items.description: The plug-in hybrid powertrain introduced on the Tourneo Custom from 2023 pairs a naturally aspirated 2.5L Atkinson-cycle petrol engine with an electric motor. Offering up to 35 miles of electric range, this PHEV system suits urban passenger transport with zero-emission capability. The engine itself is a simplified Duratec unit without turbocharging or a wet belt, making it more durable than EcoBoost alternatives but introducing hybrid-specific cooling and battery complexities. Not sure which engine size your Ford Tourneo needs? Enter your registration number above - we’ll identify the exact displacement, engine code and compatible replacement options instantly. All engines supplied through EnginesMarket include a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty from UK Ford Tourneo specialists.
items.cta: Enquire about Ford Tourneo 2.5L hybrid engine availability

fuelTypes.h2: Ford Tourneo Engines by Fuel Type
fuelTypes.intro: Choose your Ford Tourneo engine by fuel type to compare realistic replacement prices, known issues, and compatible UK supply options - from early 1.6 TDCi Connect models to the latest 2.5 PHEV Custom.

items.title: Ford Tourneo Diesel Engines
items.description: Diesel dominates the Tourneo range across two generations of Custom and Connect, with the PSA-derived DW10/DW12 and later Ford in-house EcoBlue families generating the highest replacement demand in the UK passenger transport sector.
items.descriptor: Diesel dominates the Tourneo range across two generations of Custom and Connect, with the PSA-derived DW10/DW12 and later Ford in-house EcoBlue families generating the highest replacement demand in the UK passenger transport sector.
families: DW10 - 2.0 TDCi, PSA-derived, EGR cooler failure king (Tourneo Custom 2012-2019)
DW12 - 2.2 TDCi, high-torque option, turbocharger wear (Tourneo Custom 2012-2016)
XXFA - 2.0 EcoBlue, in-house Euro 6, AdBlue crystallisation (Tourneo Custom 2019-2024)
TXWA - 2.0 EcoBlue updated, Euro 6d, SCR faults (Tourneo Custom 2020-2025)
DV5C/BHDB - 1.5 TDCi, entry-level Connect diesel, timing chain stretch (Tourneo Connect 2014-2022)
HHDA - 1.6 TDCi, early Connect unit, injector failure (Tourneo Connect 2002-2014)
foundIn: Tourneo Custom Mk8 (2012-2019) - DW10 2.0 TDCi, DW12 2.2 TDCi
Tourneo Custom Mk9 (2019-2024) - XXFA/TXWA 2.0 EcoBlue, BCFA 2.0 EcoBlue AWD
knownFor: Strong low-end torque ideal for fully loaded passenger transport
EGR and DPF failures on DW10 from stop-start school run cycles
AdBlue system crystallisation on XXFA/TXWA in urban use
Timing chain stretch on DV5C 1.5 TDCi Connect units
items.cta: Get quotes for Ford Tourneo Diesel replacement engines

items.title: Ford Tourneo Petrol Engines
items.description: Petrol power is limited to the Tourneo Connect range, dominated by the award-winning but wet belt-prone 1.0 EcoBoost three-cylinder, with a larger 1.5 EcoBoost appearing in later facelift models.
items.descriptor: Petrol power is limited to the Tourneo Connect range, dominated by the award-winning but wet belt-prone 1.0 EcoBoost three-cylinder, with a larger 1.5 EcoBoost appearing in later facelift models.
families: M1JA - 1.0 EcoBoost three-cylinder, wet belt timing, high replacement demand (Tourneo Connect 2014-2022)
M1JJ - 1.0 EcoBoost start-stop, micro-hybrid, revised belt materials (Tourneo Connect 2020-2022)
M1JA (1.5L) - 1.5 EcoBoost four-cylinder, wet belt architecture (Tourneo Connect 2022-2025)
foundIn: Tourneo Connect Mk2 facelift (2022-2025) - M1JA 1.5 EcoBoost
knownFor: Refined three-cylinder character with turbo punch
Wet belt degradation blocking oil pickup from 60,000 miles
Catastrophic engine seizure if belt fails - no warning
Lower replacement volume than diesel but higher urgency per failure
items.cta: Compare Ford Tourneo Petrol engine prices

items.title: Ford Tourneo Hybrid / Electrified Engines
items.description: The Tourneo Custom gained a plug-in hybrid option in 2023, pairing a naturally aspirated Atkinson-cycle petrol engine with an electric motor for zero-emission urban passenger duty.
items.descriptor: The Tourneo Custom gained a plug-in hybrid option in 2023, pairing a naturally aspirated Atkinson-cycle petrol engine with an electric motor for zero-emission urban passenger duty.
families: Duratec 2.5L - Atkinson-cycle petrol, no turbo, no wet belt (Tourneo Custom PHEV 2023-2025)
Electric motor provides up to 35 miles of zero-emission range
Petrol engine automatically engages for longer journeys or under heavy load
Regenerative braking charges the battery during deceleration
importantNotes: Engine itself is simpler (no turbo, no wet belt) than EcoBoost petrol units
Hybrid cooling pump failure is a known weak point
Battery condition affects overall system performance
Replacement availability more limited than diesel variants

engineYears.h2: Ford Tourneo Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section covers every major engine change across the Ford Tourneo production timeline from 2002 to the present, organised so owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt engine prices from UK specialists.

years.preview: Tourneo Connect Mk1: PSA Diesel & Petrol Launched
years.description: The original Tourneo Connect arrived as Ford's compact passenger MPV, sharing its platform with the Transit Connect van and using simple PSA-derived diesel and Ford petrol units.
keyChanges: New compact MPV based on Transit Connect platform (2002)
1.8L Duratec petrol engine added to range (2004)
Minor facelift with revised grille and interior (2008)
End of Mk1 production as Mk2 launched (2013)
knownFor: Simple, robust PSA diesel architecture with fewer emissions components
Injector failure on 1.6 TDCi after 80,000-100,000 miles
Age-related turbocharger vane sticking on high-mileage examples
Petrol 1.8 Duratec is non-interference but suffers from ignition coil failures
years.cta: Get quotes for 2002-2013 Ford Tourneo replacement engines

years.preview: Tourneo Custom Mk8 Launch: DW10 & DW12 Diesel Arrive
years.description: Ford launched the larger Tourneo Custom in 2012 as a passenger-carrying alternative to the Transit Custom van, introducing PSA-derived DW10 and DW12 diesel engines that would dominate UK replacement demand.
keyChanges: New full-size MPV based on Transit Custom platform (2012)
DW10 2.0 TDCi and DW12 2.2 TDCi diesel options at launch
First Tourneo with 9-seat passenger configuration
Zetec, Titanium and Limited trim levels introduced
knownFor: EGR cooler failure on DW10 from 60,000-90,000 miles - common replacement driver
DPF regeneration issues on stop-start passenger duty
DW12 turbocharger wastegate sticking
Robust bottom ends - failures almost always emissions or turbo-related
years.cta: Compare rebuilt DW10 and DW12 engine prices for your 2013-2014 Ford Tourneo Custom
years.ticker: Ford Tourneo Custom 2.0 TDCi engine replacement • Ford Tourneo Custom DW10 EGR failure fix • Tourneo Custom 2.2 TDCi turbo replacement cost
enquiries: Ford Tourneo Custom 2.0 TDCi engine replacement
Ford Tourneo Custom DW10 EGR failure fix
Tourneo Custom 2.2 TDCi turbo replacement cost

years.preview: Tourneo Custom Mk8 Facelift & Tourneo Connect Mk2 Launched
years.description: This period saw two major developments: the Tourneo Connect Mk2 arrived with 1.5 TDCi and 1.0 EcoBoost engines, while the Tourneo Custom received revised DW10/BNBA units and minor styling updates.
keyChanges: Tourneo Connect Mk2 launched (2014) with new 1.5 TDCi and 1.0 EcoBoost
Tourneo Custom facelift with revised front end (2015)
BNBA updated Duratorq engine replaces early DW10 in Custom (2015)
Powershift automatic option added to Custom range (2015)
Euro 6 emissions compliance phased in from late 2015
knownFor: DV5C timing chain stretch on Connect - noisy cold starts warning sign
M1JA wet belt degradation - catastrophic failure risk from 60,000 miles
BNBA revised DW10 with improved EGR cooler design but still problematic
Powershift automatic reliability concerns on high-mileage passenger fleet
years.cta: Get quotes for 2014-2018 Ford Tourneo replacement engines
years.ticker: Ford Tourneo Connect 1.0 EcoBoost wet belt failure • Tourneo Connect 1.5 TDCi timing chain replacement • Tourneo Custom BNBA EGR cooler failure
enquiries: Ford Tourneo Connect 1.0 EcoBoost wet belt failure
Tourneo Connect 1.5 TDCi timing chain replacement
Tourneo Custom BNBA EGR cooler failure

years.preview: Tourneo Custom Mk9 Launch: EcoBlue Diesel & PHEV Arrive
years.description: The third-generation Tourneo Custom arrived in 2019 with Ford's in-house 2.0 EcoBlue diesel range, replacing PSA-derived units, plus a PHEV option introduced in 2021.
keyChanges: Tourneo Custom Mk9 launched on new platform (2019)
PSA DW10/DW12 replaced by Ford EcoBlue XXFA/TXWA (2019)
2.0 EcoBlue AWD (BCFA) added for high-traction passenger duty (2020)
Plug-in hybrid 2.5 PHEV introduced (2021)
Updated SYNC infotainment and driver assistance tech
mainEngines: TXWA - 2.0L diesel EcoBlue Euro 6d (Tourneo Custom 2.0 EcoBlue 130-185hp)
BCFA - 2.0L diesel EcoBlue AWD (Tourneo Custom AWD 170-185hp)
Duratec 2.5L - PHEV petrol hybrid (Tourneo Custom PHEV)
knownFor: AdBlue crystallisation blocking injectors on XXFA/TXWA - common fault
Turbocharger wear on higher-output BCFA AWD units
PHEV offers 35-mile electric range - ideal for urban passenger transport
Generally more reliable than DW10-era engines but emissions system complexity higher
years.cta: Compare rebuilt XXFA and TXWA engine prices for your 2019-2021 Ford Tourneo Custom
years.ticker: Ford Tourneo Custom 2.0 EcoBlue AdBlue fault • Tourneo Custom BCFA turbo replacement • Tourneo Custom PHEV hybrid cooling pump failure
enquiries: Ford Tourneo Custom 2.0 EcoBlue AdBlue fault
Tourneo Custom PHEV hybrid cooling pump failure

years.preview: Tourneo Connect Mk2 Facelift & Custom Mk9 Matures
years.description: The final period sees the Tourneo Connect receive a facelift with 1.5 EcoBoost petrol, while the Tourneo Custom Mk9 continues with refined EcoBlue and PHEV powertrains.
keyChanges: Tourneo Connect facelift with revised front end (2022)
1.5 EcoBoost M1JA petrol replaces 1.0 EcoBoost in Connect (2022)
Tourneo Custom production continues with XXFA/TXWA/BCFA/PHEV (2022-2024)
Tourneo Custom Mk9 production ends (2024) with no direct successor announced
Euro 6d emissions standard fully implemented
mainEngines: XXFA / TXWA - 2.0L diesel EcoBlue (Tourneo Custom 2.0 EcoBlue)
BCFA - 2.0L diesel EcoBlue AWD (Tourneo Custom AWD)
Duratec 2.5L - PHEV petrol hybrid (Tourneo Custom PHEV)
knownFor: 1.5 EcoBoost retains wet belt architecture - same failure risk as 1.0L
Later XXFA/TXWA have improved AdBlue systems but faults still occur
PHEV remains the only electrified passenger option in range
End of Tourneo production in 2024 makes used engine market increasingly relevant

faq.h2: Ford Tourneo Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Ford Tourneo engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: A Ford Tourneo engine replacement typically costs £1,400 to £4,500 for a reconditioned or rebuilt unit supply-only, depending on engine code and variant. Used engines range from £800 to £1,800, while full supply and fit adds £400-£800.
keyPoints: 1.5 TDCi Connect engines: £1,400-£2,600 for rebuilt units
2.0 TDCi / EcoBlue Custom engines: £1,500-£3,800 supply-only
Used Tourneo engines: £800-£1,800 - verify history before buying
Professional fitting adds 8-15 hours labour plus ancillary parts
items.cta: Compare Ford Tourneo engine prices from UK specialists

items.question: Is the 1.0 EcoBoost engine in the Tourneo Connect reliable? I've heard about wet belt problems.
items.answer: The 1.0 EcoBoost has a known wet belt vulnerability - the timing belt runs inside engine oil and can degrade, blocking the oil pickup. This can cause catastrophic engine failure if not addressed preventatively.
keyPoints: Wet belt typically fails between 60,000-80,000 miles without warning
Turbocharger issues also reported from 40,000-60,000km (approx 25,000-37,000 miles)
Preventative belt replacement costs £1,200-£1,600 at a specialist
Failed belt often means full engine replacement - not just a repair
items.cta: Get quotes for 1.0 EcoBoost wet belt replacement or rebuilt engine

items.question: Which Ford Tourneo engine is most reliable - petrol or diesel?
items.answer: The 2.0 TDCi DW10 and later EcoBlue diesels are the most popular and generally robust, though emissions components (EGR, DPF, AdBlue) cause issues. The 1.5 TDCi is solid but suffers timing chain stretch. Petrol EcoBoost has wet belt risks but fewer emissions problems.
keyPoints: What Car? ranks Ford 17th out of 32 manufacturers for reliability (2023 survey)
2.0 TDCi diesel has strong bottom end - EGR/DPF are the main failure points
1.5 TDCi Connect: good engine but timing chain can stretch - listen for cold start rattle
1.0 EcoBoost: refined drive but wet belt is the single biggest risk
items.cta: Compare Ford Tourneo diesel and petrol engine prices

items.question: How do I find the exact engine code in my Ford Tourneo?
items.answer: Your Ford Tourneo's engine code is stamped on the type plate and visible in the VIN. The 8th character of your 17-digit VIN often indicates the engine code, or check the type plate on the B-pillar inside the driver's door frame.
keyPoints: Engine code is on the type plate - located on B-pillar when front door is open
VIN is visible through windscreen viewing window - 8th character often denotes engine
Common Tourneo codes: DW10, DW12, BNBA, XXFA, DV5C, M1JA
Enter your registration on our tool - we decode engine code instantly
items.cta: Enter your reg to identify your Ford Tourneo engine code

items.question: Should I buy a used, reconditioned or remanufactured Ford Tourneo engine?
items.answer: Used (£800-£1,500) is cheapest but risky - unknown history. Reconditioned (£1,400-£3,200) balances cost and reliability with new wear parts. Remanufactured (£2,200-£4,500) is factory-spec with all components replaced - best for long-term ownership.
keyPoints: Used engines: fast and cheap but warranty limited - verify compression test reports
Reconditioned: stripped, inspected, worn parts replaced - typically 12-month warranty
Remanufactured: zero-hour engine, upgraded components, 12-24 month warranty
Supply and fit packages include labour warranty - recommended for peace of mind
items.cta: Compare reconditioned, rebuilt and used Ford Tourneo engine prices

items.question: How long does it take to fit a replacement Ford Tourneo engine?
items.answer: Professional Ford Tourneo engine fitting typically takes 8 to 15 hours of workshop labour. Including engine delivery, ancillary parts replacement and testing, expect 2-5 days from order to driving away, depending on supplier and workshop availability.
keyPoints: Labour time: 8-15 hours for a standard engine swap
Additional time for dual-mass flywheel, clutch, turbo oil lines if replaced
Many suppliers offer supply and fit - single invoice, guaranteed installation
Urgent? Some specialists offer express service - ask when getting quotes
items.cta: Find Ford Tourneo engine supply and fit deals near you

items.question: What warranty comes with a reconditioned Ford Tourneo engine in the UK?
items.answer: Most reconditioned and remanufactured Ford Tourneo engines come with a 12-month unlimited mileage warranty as standard. Some premium suppliers offer 24-month coverage. Used engines may have 30-90 day warranties - always check terms before purchase.
keyPoints: Reconditioned engines: typically 12-month unlimited mileage parts warranty
Remanufactured engines: often 12-24 months - ask about labour coverage
Used engines: limited warranty (30-90 days) - read exclusions carefully
Supply and fit packages may include labour warranty - valuable protection
items.cta: Get a warranty-backed Ford Tourneo replacement engine quote

items.question: Can I fit a Ford Tourneo engine myself to save money?
items.answer: Yes, you can buy a used or reconditioned engine and fit it yourself, but this risks voiding the warranty and may hide costly issues. Most suppliers require professional installation for warranty validity - DIY fitting is only recommended for experienced mechanics.
keyPoints: Self-fitting may void supplier warranty - check terms before purchase
Modern Tourneo engines need ECU calibration - specialist diagnostics required
Ancillary parts (flywheel, clutch, turbo lines) often need replacement simultaneously
Professional fitting costs £400-£800 - often worth it for warranty protection
items.cta: Compare Ford Tourneo engine prices with or without fitting

items.question: What are the common Ford Tourneo 2.0 TDCi engine problems I should watch for?
items.answer: The 2.0 TDCi (DW10/BNBA) in Tourneo Custom suffers three main issues: EGR cooler clogging from 60,000-90,000 miles, DPF regeneration failure on stop-start passenger duty, and turbocharger wear on higher-mileage units. EcoBlue (XXFA) adds AdBlue crystallisation problems.
keyPoints: EGR cooler failure: loss of power, coolant loss - replace cooler immediately
DPF clogging: limp mode from short journeys - forced regen may clear it early
Turbo failure: whistling noise, blue smoke - catch early or debris destroys engine
AdBlue faults (XXFA/TXWA): crystallised injector - common on urban passenger fleet
items.cta: Get quotes for Ford Tourneo 2.0 TDCi EGR, DPF or turbo repairs

items.question: Is it worth replacing the engine in my Ford Tourneo or should I scrap the van?
items.answer: Replacing a Ford Tourneo engine is worth it if the body and passenger conversion are in good condition. A rebuilt engine with warranty costs £1,800-£3,500 supplied and fitted - far less than replacing a wheelchair-accessible or 9-seat MPV worth £6,000-£15,000.
keyPoints: Compare rebuilt engine cost against vehicle value - typically worth it if repair <60% of value
Wheelchair-accessible conversions often justify engine replacement regardless of age
A quality rebuilt engine can deliver 100,000+ miles of reliable service
Selling as non-runner loses thousands - replacement preserves asset value
items.cta: Compare Ford Tourneo engine replacement costs vs vehicle value
